Artifact viewer · Foundation dossier

The Foundation, on letterhead.

Five Texas First Bank verification letters document $2,065,367.96 in deposits to S4TF, LTD between March 2013 and 29 June 2018. The architectural deck, the Foundation Coin, and the revenue chart are reproduced alongside.

Operator: 9th Ward Production and Promotions Company, LLC Funded through: S4TF, LTD
§ 01 Bank-letterhead evidence

The financial spine.

Five letters, signed by an Assistant Vice President of the bank, at five separate dates over three years. Cumulative deposits 2013 through 29 June 2018: $2,065,367.96. Average annual deposits 2013\u20132017: $386,638.56. Average monthly deposits: $32,219.88. That is not a forecast. It is the bank\u2019s own ledger, in writing, on letterhead.

  • Bank letter Texas First Bank — verification letter, deposits 2013–2015 ↗ 18 November 2015

    Re: S4TF, LTD, account #10016236. Total deposits 2013 (Mar 14–Dec 31): $287,830.79. 2014: $391,629.85. 2015 (Jan–Oct 31): $365,699.25. Signed Billie Jean Higginbotham, Banking Center Manager / AVP.

  • Bank letter Texas First Bank — verification letter, full year 2015 ↗ 6 January 2016

    Re: S4TF, LTD. Total deposits 2015: $435,490.20.

  • Bank letter Texas First Bank — verification letter, full year 2016 ↗ 18 January 2017

    Re: S4TF, LTD. Total deposits 2016: $438,776.85.

  • Bank letter Texas First Bank — verification letter, Jan–Nov 2017 ↗ 6 December 2017

    Re: S4TF. Total deposits January–November 2017: $368,107.35.

  • Bank letter Texas First Bank — cumulative verification letter, 2013–June 2018 ↗ 29 June 2018

    Re: S4TF, LTD. Cumulative deposits 2013 through 29 June 2018: $2,065,367.96. Average annual deposits 2013–2017: $386,638.56. Average monthly deposits: $32,219.88.

§ 02 The architectural deck

The Foundation Project.

The 45-page Prezi-export deck published 2017\u20132018. The flagship sited in El Paso, TX, operated through 9th Ward Production and Promotions Company, LLC, structured around a 100 Indie Artists / 100 Major Celebrities / City of El Paso constituency, with a 50 / 50 ownership architecture and serialized fractional tokens issued against it.

PDF · 45 pages The Foundation Project — architectural deck ↗ 2017–2018

45-page Prezi-export, 2017–2018. Sections: The Plan, Who, What, Feasibility, Key Partners, How it Works, First Steps. Documents the El Paso flagship, the 100 Indie Artists / 100 Major Celebrities / City of El Paso constituency, the 50/50 ownership architecture, the V.I.P. access model, and the multi-vertical revenue plan. Contact of record at end of deck: Kirbie Speights.

§ 03 Token and revenue chart

The coin and the chart.

The Foundation Coin is the serialized fractional-ownership token issued against the El Paso flagship; serial 0001, mint code EP-FLAG-999. The revenue chart from the deck visualises the same deposit history the bank letters confirm.

  • Image Foundation Coin — EP-FLAG-999, 0001 ↗ 2017

    Serialized fractional-ownership token rendered for the El Paso flagship. Obverse: “Our Unity Is Our Strength” rim, “the foundation” wordmark on teal field. Reverse: “Together We Build!” rim, five-point star centered, serial 0001, mint code EP-FLAG-999.

  • Image S4TF revenue trajectory, 2013–2017 ↗ 2017

    Annual revenue chart from the Foundation deck. Bars peak in 2015–2016 above the $400,000 line and step down in 2017 — a partial-year figure shown alongside the full prior years.

§ 04 Independent corroboration

The 2011 print record.

Six years before the bank-letter window opens, Go Local Magazine, Volume I, Edition 2 (April 2011) profiles the property at 100 Carnarvon Drive, Houston \u2014 a 36,000-square-foot residence built by Lee Calloway, originally specified at approximately $1,500 per square foot, with a ten-karat gold-leaf ceiling and roughly nine million dollars in antiquities \u2014 and identifies it explicitly as the headquarters of the Power In Numbers Economic Development Initiative. The full reproduction is embedded on the Go Local entry.

  • Track record · 2017 The Foundation \u2014 community-owned venue The full essay, with foil mapping to Venu Holding Corporation (NYSE: VENU) and the seven-year head start.
  • Track record · 2010 Go Local Magazine Volume I, Edition 2 (April 2011) names 100 Carnarvon Drive in print as the firm\u2019s headquarters.
  • Case study Ghana market entry The same principal threaded forward fifteen years \u2014 from the 2011 Go Local profile to the 2026 Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol engagement.
Power In Numbers A strategic consultancy. Practiced as decision cartography.
